message:
fix(auth): make auth.json write crash-safe by padding before truncation

Prevent corrupt auth.json if the process crashes between seek(0) and
set_len(0) — the old sequence (truncate then write) left a zero-length
file window where a crash would lose all credentials.

New approach:
1. Record old file length before seeking to start
2. Write new data at position 0 (overwrites in-place)
3. If new data is shorter, pad with spaces (valid JSON whitespace) to
   cover the old trailing bytes — this ensures the file always contains
   valid JSON even if we crash before truncation
4. Truncate to exact new length only after padding is flushed

This eliminates the data-loss window: at every point during the write,
the file contains either the old valid JSON or the new valid JSON
(potentially with trailing whitespace, which JSON parsers ignore).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
